meta.title: AI Tools for Teens (13+): Prompting, Creative Projects & Safe AI Use
meta.description: A 6-month AI course for teens aged 13+: prompt engineering, AI image, audio, video and code tools, real projects, and how to use AI honestly. Every tool is one a 13+ student can actually sign up for.
meta.level: Complete Beginner to Confident AI User

program_overview.title: AI Tools Mastery for Teens - Creative Intelligence
program_overview.tagline: From AI Curious to AI Creator - Master the Future Today
program_overview.description:
This revolutionary 6-month program transforms teens into confident AI users and creators. Students will master prompt engineering, explore AI tools chosen for their age, create amazing projects, and develop critical thinking about AI's role in society.

Perfect for ages 13-18, this course covers everything from ChatGPT conversations to creating AI art, music, videos, games, and even AI-assisted coding. Students will build an impressive portfolio while learning AI safety, ethics, and future-ready skills.
what_makes_this_different: Plain-language approach to complex AI concepts
Ethical AI understanding from day one
No coding required (but coding AI tools included)

month_1.title: Month 1: AI Fundamentals & ChatGPT Mastery
month_1.description: Understand AI basics, master ChatGPT, and learn the art of prompt engineering.

week_1.title: Introduction to AI World
topics: What is Artificial Intelligence? Simple explanation
AI in everyday life: Siri, Alexa, YouTube recommendations
How AI learns: Machine Learning basics for teens
Types of AI: Narrow AI vs General AI
AI history: From calculators to ChatGPT
AI vocabulary: Model, training, dataset, algorithm
Setting up safe AI accounts with parental guidance
week_1.safety_focus: Understanding AI is a tool, not a replacement for thinking
week_1.practice: Spot five places AI helped you this week, like YouTube suggestions or a voice assistant, and mark which one felt the smartest.

week_2.title: ChatGPT Fundamentals
projects: Plan a birthday party with AI
week_2.safety_focus: Never share personal information, always verify important facts
week_2.practice: With a parent nearby, ask ChatGPT three questions about your favorite animal and note which answer surprised you most.

week_3.title: Prompt Engineering Basics
week_3.practice: Rewrite one vague prompt like 'draw a dog' into a detailed prompt with a place, a mood and a style, then compare the two answers.

week_4.title: Advanced ChatGPT Techniques
week_4.practice: Hold a five turn conversation where you teach the AI about your favorite hobby, then ask it to quiz you on it.

month_2.title: Month 2: AI Art & Image Generation
month_2.description: Master AI image generation tools, create stunning artwork, and understand visual AI.

week_5.title: Introduction to AI Art
week_5.techniques: Basic prompt structure for images: subject, style, mood, details
week_5.practice: Describe your dream bedroom in words, generate an image from the description and circle two details the AI got right.

week_6.title: AI Image Generation Mastery
week_6.practice: Generate the same scene in three styles, cartoon, realistic and abstract, and write down which prompt words worked best.

week_7.title: Midjourney and Advanced AI Art
week_7.practice: Write one /imagine prompt that uses an aspect ratio parameter and one style word, then save your favorite result.

week_8.title: AI Photo Editing and Manipulation
week_8.practice: With permission, remove the background from a family photo using an AI editor and place the subject somewhere fun.

month_3.title: Month 3: AI Music, Audio & Video Creation
month_3.description: Explore AI tools for music composition, audio editing, and video creation.

week_9.title: AI Music Generation
topics: Genres and styles in AI music
projects: Create theme song for imaginary show
week_9.exercises: Generate 20 different musical styles and moods
week_9.practice: Generate two short tunes in different genres and tell someone at home which mood each one gives you.

week_10.title: AI Voice and Audio Tools
week_10.practice: Turn a favorite poem or joke into speech with a text to speech tool using two different character voices.

week_11.title: AI Video Generation
week_11.skills: Storyboarding, script writing, video editing basics
week_11.practice: Storyboard a 15 second video in three drawn frames, then create one of the scenes with an AI video tool.

week_12.title: AI Animation and Motion
week_12.showcase: Animation festival with all students' work
week_12.practice: Animate a simple character face showing happy, sad and surprised using an AI animation tool, and show your family the result.

month_4.title: Month 4: AI for Learning & Productivity
month_4.description: Use AI tools to enhance learning, boost productivity, and excel in academics.

week_13.title: AI Study Assistants
week_13.ethics: Using AI to learn, not to cheat
week_13.practice: Use an AI study helper to practice five maths or spelling questions, and mark which ones you solved without any help.

week_14.title: AI Research and Writing Tools
week_14.practice: Ask AI to summarize a short article about space or animals, then check one fact from the summary in another source.

week_15.title: AI Productivity Tools
week_15.practice: Plan your perfect Saturday with an AI generated schedule, then fix two things it got wrong about you.

week_16.title: AI for Creative Projects
week_16.practice: Brainstorm ten silly story ideas with AI, pick your favorite and write the opening paragraph yourself.

month_5.title: Month 5: AI Coding, Apps & Advanced Tools
month_5.description: Learn AI-assisted coding, app development, and explore advanced AI platforms.

week_17.title: AI Coding Assistants
projects: Build first website with AI help
week_17.languages: HTML/CSS, Python, JavaScript basics with AI assistance
week_17.practice: Ask an AI assistant to explain a small piece of code in plain words, then change one thing and predict what happens.

week_18.title: No-Code AI App Builders
week_18.practice: Sketch the screens of a simple app idea on paper and list the three buttons it would need.

week_19.title: Machine Learning Platforms
week_19.concepts: Training data, testing data, accuracy, confusion matrix
week_19.practice: Train a Teachable Machine model to recognize two of your toys and test it five times with each toy.

week_20.title: AI Game Development
week_20.practice: Design an NPC for your dream game: give it a name, one behavior rule and one line of dialogue written with AI help.

month_6.title: Month 6: AI Projects & Future Skills
month_6.description: Create advanced projects, understand AI's future, and prepare for AI-driven careers.

week_21.title: AI for Social Good
week_21.practice: Pick one problem in your neighborhood and write three ways an AI helper could make it better.

week_22.title: AI Business and Entrepreneurship
topics: AI startup ideas for young entrepreneurs
week_22.practice: Invent a small business idea, generate a logo for it with AI and write its one line slogan yourself.

week_23.title: AI Safety and Ethics
week_23.discussions: Group debates on AI ethics scenarios
week_23.practice: Play detective with a parent: compare one real photo and one AI image and list two clues that gave the AI image away.

week_24.title: Future of AI and Emerging Tech
topics: AI agents that use tools on your behalf
week_24.practice: Draw or describe a robot helper for the year 2040 and label three sensors it would need to do its job.

week_25.title: Capstone Project Development
week_25.practice: Choose your capstone idea, list the AI tools you will combine and write the build steps in order.

week_26.title: Graduation and Future Planning
week_26.practice: Practice your final presentation out loud once and prepare a clear answer to 'which parts did the AI do and which parts did you do?'

general_rules: Never share personal information (name, address, school, phone)
Always use AI with adult supervision initially
Verify important information from multiple sources
Don't use AI to complete homework dishonestly

ages_8_10: Focus on creative and educational tools
Emphasis on AI as a helper tool

parental_controls: Set up family accounts where available

immediate_opportunities: AI content creation for social media
Tutoring other teens in AI tools

who_is_this_for.working_professionals: Teachers and parents learning alongside their teenager
who_is_this_for.freelancers: Future AI content creators and developers
who_is_this_for.creative_teens: Creative teenagers who want to use AI tools well
who_is_this_for.anyone: Anyone passionate about using AI creatively

course_guarantees.live_classes: Live, interactive classes with a real instructor, never pre-recorded videos.
course_guarantees.small_batches: Small batches only: group classes are capped at 10 students, with mini-batch (3 to 4 students) and personal 1-on-1 options.
course_guarantees.structured_curriculum: A structured, well-paced curriculum taught step by step, with hands-on practice in every session.
course_guarantees.doubt_support: Doubt support between classes over WhatsApp, so you are never left stuck.
course_guarantees.certificate: A course-completion certificate you can share.
course_guarantees.free_demo: A free demo class before you enrol, so you can decide with no pressure.

faqs.question: What age is appropriate for this AI tools course for teens?
faqs.answer: This course is for ages 13 to 18. The age floor is not arbitrary: most AI tools set 13 as their own minimum in their terms of use, so a younger student cannot lawfully hold an account. Every tool in this course is one a 13+ student can actually sign up for. If your child is under 13, ask us about our AI courses built for that age group instead.

faqs.question: Is it safe for my teenager to use AI tools?
faqs.answer: Safety is built into the course, not bolted on. We teach it from day one: never share personal information, always verify what the AI tells you, understand what it cannot do, and know when not to use it at all. We also check each tool's own age requirement before we teach it, and we tell students plainly which tools they are not old enough for yet.

faqs.question: Does my teenager need any prior experience to start this course?
faqs.answer: No prior experience is needed! We start from the very basics of what AI is and how it works. The course is designed for complete beginners while also challenging those who already have some familiarity with AI tools.

faqs.question: Will my teenager learn actual skills or just play with AI toys?
faqs.answer: This is a comprehensive skill-building program! Students learn prompt engineering, critical thinking, creative problem-solving, digital literacy, and ethical reasoning. They work with professional AI tools used in real industries, from ChatGPT to Midjourney to coding assistants.

faqs.question: How will this course help my teenager's future career?
faqs.answer: AI literacy is becoming essential across all careers. This course prepares students for the AI-driven future with skills in prompt engineering, AI ethics, creative AI applications, and critical evaluation of AI outputs. These skills are already in high demand across industries.

faqs.question: What equipment does my teenager need for this AI course?
faqs.answer: A basic computer or laptop with internet connection is sufficient. Some tools work on tablets too. All software used is either free or has free educational versions. We guide parents on setting up safe accounts with appropriate controls.

related_courses.title: AI & Machine Learning for Teens: Zero to AI Expert
related_courses.description: Go from using AI tools to building your own models.

related_courses.title: Python Programming for Teens: Zero to Advanced in 2 Years
related_courses.description: Learn the language most AI is actually written in.

related_courses.title: DSA & Problem Solving for Teens: Algorithms to Interviews
related_courses.description: Build the problem-solving muscle AI cannot do for you.

why_this_course.title: Why AI Literacy is the Most Important Skill for Teens Today
paragraphs: AI is transforming every industry, every career, and every aspect of life. Teens who master AI tools today will have an unprecedented advantage in tomorrow's world. This isn't about replacing human skills, it's about amplifying them with the most powerful tools ever created.

From creating stunning artwork to writing compelling content, from coding applications to solving complex problems, AI tools are becoming as essential as knowing how to use a computer. Students learn to use 50+ real AI tools while developing critical thinking about AI's capabilities and limitations.

Beyond tools, this course builds the ethical framework and critical thinking skills needed to navigate an AI-powered world responsibly. Our graduates don't just use AI, they understand it, question it, and shape how it's used for good.
highlights: Confident, safe use of the AI tools you are actually old enough to use
Prompt engineering skills that transfer to any AI platform
Safety-first approach with ethical AI understanding
Real projects published and shared publicly
Portfolio that impresses schools and opens opportunities

projects_throughout_course: Month 1 (Weeks 1-4): AI chatbot mastery, prompt engineering projects
Month 2 (Weeks 5-8): AI art generation, image editing, creative portfolio
Month 3 (Weeks 9-12): AI music, video, and animation creation projects
Month 4 (Weeks 13-16): AI study tools, productivity apps, creative projects
Month 5 (Weeks 17-20): AI coding assistants, no-code app building, ML platforms
Month 6 (Weeks 21-26): AI for social good, capstone project, future skills
skills_mastered: AI Chat: ChatGPT and Perplexity - prompt engineering and conversation design
AI Art: Midjourney, Bing Image Creator, Leonardo.ai, AI photo editing
AI Music & Audio: AI music generation, voice tools, audio creation
AI Video: AI video generation, animation, motion graphics
AI Productivity: study assistants, research tools, writing helpers
AI Coding: AI coding assistants, no-code app builders, ML platforms
AI Ethics: safety, bias awareness, responsible AI use
Prompt Engineering: writing effective prompts for any AI tool

weekly_structure.ai_exploration: 1-2 hours experimenting with new AI tools

certification.final_certificate: Certified AI Creator for Youth (Modern Age Coders)
certification.capstone_required: Multi-tool AI project combining art, text, audio, and video AI
certification.portfolio: 50+ AI-created projects in digital showcase portfolio

support_provided.live_instruction: Expert-led live classes with recorded replays
support_provided.safe_ai_access: All AI tools used in supervised, age-appropriate settings
support_provided.parent_updates: Weekly progress reports and tool usage guidelines
support_provided.community: Access to Modern Age Coders AI creators community
support_provided.ethics_focus: Dedicated module on AI safety, ethics, and responsible use

prerequisites.experience: No prior AI or coding experience needed
prerequisites.equipment: Computer or tablet with internet connection
prerequisites.software: Most AI tools used are free or have free tiers - guidance provided
prerequisites.curiosity: Interest in AI, creativity, and building cool things
